title: Audit Logs in CloudDash
content:
CloudDash audit logs provide a tamper-evident record of all security-relevant actions within your organization, supporting compliance requirements for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and internal security reviews.

Availability: Audit logs are available on Enterprise plans by default and as a $150/month add-on for Pro plans. Logs are not available on Starter plans.

Recorded Events: User authentication (login, logout, SSO, failed attempts), team management (invite, remove, role change), API key lifecycle (create, rotate, revoke), dashboard and alert CRUD operations, integration changes (connect, disconnect, credential update), billing actions (plan change, payment method update), SSO configuration changes, and data export requests.

Log Entry Format: Each entry contains timestamp (UTC), actor (user email or API key ID), action (e.g., dashboard.created), resource_type, resource_id, resource_name, IP address, user agent, and change details (before/after values for modifications).

Accessing Audit Logs: Settings > Security > Audit Logs. Filter by date range, actor, action type, or resource. Export logs as CSV or JSON for external SIEM ingestion. Enterprise customers can stream logs in real-time to Splunk, Datadog, or AWS S3 via the Audit Log Streaming integration.

Retention: Pro add-on retains 90 days of audit logs. Enterprise retains 13 months by default, extendable to 7 years for compliance requirements. Logs are immutable — no user, including Admins, can modify or delete audit log entries.

Compliance Reports: Enterprise plans include pre-built compliance reports: Access Review (all users and their roles), Privileged Actions (Admin-level changes in last 30 days), and Failed Authentication Summary. Schedule automated report delivery to compliance team email addresses.
